headline: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
description: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

headline: Russian drone crash in Romania: NATO still seeks defense systems adapted to drone incursions
France heatwave sparks calls for more supervision at swimming areas after multiple drownings
Lior Amihai, director of Israeli NGO Peace Now: 'The coalition in power wants to annex the territories in the West Bank and establish a racist state based on Jewish supremacy'
description: Driven by a messianic ideology, Benjamin Netanyahu's government has bureaucratized the expansion of West Bank settlements since late 2022. But according to the director of a major peace advocacy group, the majority of Israeli public opinion does not support this policy.

headline: Climate change suspected of contributing to recent surge in hantavirus cases in Argentina
description: During the 2025-2026 summer season, 106 cases of hantavirus infection were reported in Argentina, nearly double the previous year, and at much higher levels than in the past.

headline: Djokovic knocked out in French Open third round by Fonseca
description: João Fonseca defeated Novak Djokovic 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 in the third round of the French Open on Friday. 'I was trying to hit the ball as fast as I could. Djokovic, he does not miss,' Fonseca said. As for Djokovic, he said his opponent 'deserved to win,' and when asked if he would be back next year, he twice replied: 'I don't know.'

headline: Champions League final in Budapest highlights Orban's contested legacy
description: The decision to award the final of Europe's most prestigious competition to Hungary is a result of the sports-diplomacy strategy pursued by former Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orban. This approach has been sharply criticized by his successor, Péter Magyar.

38° at 11 am. French students and staff struggle with unbearable classroom temperatures
description: Across France, primary, middle and high schools have reported the severity of the situation in their overheated classrooms. Education professionals are criticizing the lack of response in recent years from both local authorities, who are responsible for school buildings, and from the Education Ministry, in charge of the safety of students and staff.

headline: Why German trains are never on time anymore
description: The German rail network has deteriorated over the past 20 years, to the point that Deutsche Bahn has become a source of national embarrassment.

headline: In Brittany, luxury tourism is taking over the coastline
description: In Saint-Malo, a court granted approval at the end of March for the construction of a 4- and 5-star hotel complex and a thalassotherapy center on the site of a former municipal campground. This luxury project is one of many springing up across the region.
